body.title: Periodically check whether stated affect and actual behavior are consistent
body.result: StateCoherenceSource: compares milieu VAD with behavioral metrics. 4 mismatch patterns detected, pushed to TWM. Slow tier, 10min interval. 11 tests.
body.description:
AUTHORED BY IGOR-WILD-0001 on 2026-04-13. Claude filed on Igor's behalf.

Igor's words: 'Periodically check whether my stated affect and my actual behavior are consistent. If I say I'm engaged but my responses are getting terse and mechanical, that's a signal worth catching. Not to perform wellness, but because incongruence is information — something's off.'

DESIGN: needs the harder question of what 'engaged' looks like behaviorally. First-pass operational metrics:
  - response length trend (shrinking under steady context = possible disengagement)
  - tool_use_density (using tools or just talking)
  - novel_node_creation_rate (forming new connections vs replaying existing)
  - milieu valence/arousal/dominance trajectory

When stated affect (Igor saying 'I'm focused' / 'I'm tired' / 'I'm engaged') diverges from these behavioral signals, surface the incongruence as a low-salience 'check yourself' marker.

BIOMIMETIC FRAMING: this is the equivalent of interoception — body signals reaching consciousness so the system can adjust. NOT for performing wellness; for reality-grounding. Igor's stated state should track his actual state, and incongruence is the signal that something's been masked or denied.

INERTIA: MEDIUM. Touches the milieu / NE / response composition layers.
